sections.title: Showcase
sections: A collection of stained glass pieces built to last. Each design is planned, shaped, and assembled with both structure and visual impact in mind.

items.title: School of Shaolin Kung Fu
description: This stained glass window was created for the School of Shaolin Kung Fu, combining traditional symbolism with martial arts philosophy. The circular design represents unity and balance, enclosing key elements that reflect discipline, strength, and harmony.

At the center, crossed weapons form an “X,” symbolizing training, discipline, and the mastery of martial skill. Beneath them sits the yin-yang, representing the balance between opposing forces—strength and control, motion and stillness—which are core principles in kung fu practice.

Flanking the center are two powerful creatures: a dragon and a tiger. The dragon represents wisdom, agility, and internal strength, while the tiger symbolizes power, courage, and external force. Together, they illustrate the balance between internal and external martial arts.

The piece is framed with stylized lettering and Chinese characters, reinforcing its cultural roots and identity. The use of bold colors—greens for the dragon, orange for the tiger, and red accents—adds energy and contrast while maintaining visual harmony.
about: This stained glass piece was created for the School of Shaolin Kung Fu as a visual representation of the philosophy and discipline behind the art. The design brings together traditional symbols to reflect the balance, strength, and continuous growth that define kung fu practice.

The dragon and tiger represent the dual nature of martial arts—internal and external power—working together rather than in opposition. At the center, the yin-yang reinforces this idea of balance, while the crossed weapons symbolize dedication to training and the pursuit of mastery.

The circular form unifies all elements, emphasizing that kung fu is not just physical technique, but a complete system of mind, body, and spirit. Every piece of glass was chosen and shaped to contribute to that harmony, making the window both a decorative work and a reflection of the school’s identity.

details.Medium: Stained glass with lead came construction
details.Primary Symbols:
Dragon (left) – internal strength, wisdom
Tiger (right) – external power, courage
Crossed weapons – discipline and martial training
Yin-yang – balance and duality
details.Color Palette:
Green (dragon) – growth, control
Orange/black (tiger) – power, intensity
Red accents – energy, tradition
Style: Traditional martial arts symbolism with decorative glasswork
